plan:
- Initialize project with `npm init -y` and install dependencies (express, supertest, etc.).
- Create the directory structure with `src/` and `test/` directories.
- Implement `src/app.js` to initialize Express and set up basic middleware.
- Create service files for each domain (projects, tasks, members, comments) with CRUD operations.
- Create route files for each domain to handle HTTP requests.
- Implement error handling middleware for 400, 409, etc.
- Write the test file `test/oracle.test.js` based on the provided specification.
- Implement the required endpoints exactly as specified in the test.
- Use `node:crypto` for generating unique IDs.
- Use in-memory storage (arrays) for simplicity.
- Write unit tests for each service and route.
- Run the tests and fix any issues.
- Ensure the app exports correctly for testing.

Files:
- `package.json`: Project configuration and dependencies.
- `src/app.js`: Main app file, initializes Express, sets up middleware, and mounts routes.
- `src/routes/projects.js`: Routes for projects (GET, POST).
- `src/routes/tasks.js`: Routes for tasks (POST, GET, PATCH).
- `src/routes/members.js`: Routes for members (POST, GET).
- `src/routes/comments.js`: Routes for comments (POST, GET).
- `src/services/projects.js`: Service for projects (CRUD operations).
- `src/services/tasks.js`: Service for tasks.
- `src/services/members.js`: Service for members.
- `src/services/comments.js`: Service for comments.
- `src/errors.js`: Custom error handling.
- `test/oracle.test.js`: Test cases based on the specification.

Checkpoints:
- Build: Ensure the project structure and dependencies are correct.
- Test: Run the test file with `node --test oracle.test.js` and ensure all tests pass.
